rows.title: IRDAI Issues Revised Information and Cyber Security Guidelines for All Insurers and Intermediaries
rows.summary:
The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India has issued the IRDAI Information and Cyber Security Guidelines, 2026, replacing its 2023 framework and setting revised minimum standards and governance requirements applicable to all regulated entities in the Indian insurance sector.

On 6 April 2026, the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) published Circular Ref No. IRDAI/GA&HR/CIR/MISC/51/4/2026, addressed to all insurers, insurance intermediaries and the Insurance Information Bureau of India (IIB). The circular supersedes the earlier guidelines issued under Circular Ref IRDAI/GA&HR/GDL/MISC/88/04/2023 dated 24 April 2023. IRDAI states that no financial penalty is associated with this guidance instrument.

IRDAI states that the revised guidelines have been issued in view of the evolving threat landscape, industry feedback, and recommendations from various IRDAI committees. The authority finds that the updated framework is intended to enable the insurance industry to further strengthen its defences and related governance mechanisms to address emerging cyber threats. The guidelines, attached as Annexure B to the circular, set out the minimum standards and governance mechanisms to be adopted by all regulated entities, with a summary of major changes from the prior framework provided separately as Annexure A.

IRDAI states that all insurers, including Foreign Reinsurance Branches, and all insurance intermediaries, covering brokers, corporate agents, web aggregators, third-party administrators, insurance marketing firms, insurance repositories, insurance self-network platforms, corporate surveyors, motor insurance service providers and common service centres, along with the IIB, are required to strictly adhere to the guidelines and ensure compliance from the current financial year. The circular was issued under the signature of A. R. Nithiyanantham, Executive Director (GA&HR), at IRDAI's offices in the Financial District, Hyderabad.
